content.problemStatement:
Cycle in a Linked List  (5 Marks)You are given a linked list **A** of size **N**.

Return the node where the cycle begins in the linked list.

If there is no cycle, return **None**.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer **T**, the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer **N**, the length of the linked list.
The second line contains **N** space-separated integers

`A1 A2 A3 ... AN` representing the values of the linked list nodes.
The third line contains an integer **pos**, denoting the index (0-based) of the node where the cycle starts.

If `pos = -1`, it means there is no cycle.






Output Format

For each test case:

Print the value of the node where the cycle begins.
If there is no cycle, print `NULL`.
